allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory helps anchoring work well by using familiar schemas to reduce working-memory burden during early orientation.
Narratives gives anchoring a memorable context that can orient attention before facts are unpacked in detail.
allies.source_quote: Stories are highly effective anchors because they lodge in memory easily and get into the brain faster than raw data or statistics.

antagonists.rationale_text: Critical-thinking pushes back on anchoring by demanding that the first frame stay corrigible in the face of new evidence.
antagonists.source_quote: Anchoring is diametrically opposed to critical thinking, which demands questioning one's own biases and continually updating beliefs based on new data.

antagonists.rationale_text: Base-rates counters anchoring by replacing the inside-view frame with an outside-view reference point grounded in broader outcomes.
antagonists.source_quote: decision-making should be anchored instead to the outside view, focusing on general outcomes and external data

structured_tensions.tension_text: Anchoring conflicts with critical-thinking when the first frame becomes sticky enough to block real belief revision.
structured_tensions.source_quote: Anchoring is diametrically opposed to critical thinking, which demands questioning one's own biases and continually updating beliefs based on new data.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on two clean allies and a strong antagonistic relationship to critical-thinking and outside-view correction.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor persuasion relation sprawl beyond the source-backed memory, orientation, and anti-entrenchment surface.
open_questions: Pyramid-principle and framing are source-real allies, but they were omitted because the current graph vocabulary does not expose them as clean target ids.
